summary: Replace computeGraphConnectivity with computeGraphReachability using typed BFS and adaptive weight fallback
context: The old graph scoring did substring entity matching + 1-hop BFS overlap counting at 10% weight. The graph's typed relations were wasted. Increasing weight to 35% risks breaking retrieval for users with empty graphs.
rationale: Typed BFS traversal follows specific relation types (decided_by, affects, depends_on, etc.) with depth-based path scoring and relation type bonuses. Adaptive weight fallback redistributes the 35% graph weight proportionally when all graph scores are 0, preventing score compression for sparse/empty graphs. Relevance paths annotate assembled decisions with how they were found.

alternatives.option: Keep old computeGraphConnectivity at higher weight
alternatives.reason_rejected: Substring matching + neighbor overlap doesn't leverage typed relations — misses structurally connected decisions
35% of score would be zero for upgrading users with empty graphs, compressing effective score range
